title: Women's Chikdril Gold Cup 2026: Munsel Crowned Champions
author: TNSA Media Team
content: The Women's Chikdril Gold Cup 2026 concluded with Munsel claiming the championship title in an exciting final held at Mundgod.
Longsho put up a strong fight throughout the tournament but finished as runners-up after a competitive final match.

The tournament spanned several days, showcasing the growing talent and competitive spirit in women's football across Tibetan settlements. The event featured teams competing in group stages followed by knockout rounds.
The Chikdril Gold Cup continues to grow as one of the premier women's football events in the Tibetan sports calendar, providing a platform for female athletes to compete at a high level.

title: 32nd Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup Announced
author: TNSA
content: The Tibetan National Sports Association (TNSA) has officially announced the 32nd edition of the Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup (GCMGC), the most prestigious football tournament in the Tibetan diaspora community.
Applications are now open for teams wishing to host the tournament. Interested settlements and communities are encouraged to submit their hosting proposals before the announced deadline.

The GCMGC has been a cornerstone of Tibetan sports culture for over three decades, bringing together teams from settlements across India, Nepal, and beyond. The tournament honors the memory of the Great Mother and serves as a unifying event for the Tibetan community.

Registration details and team slots are being finalized. Teams are advised to register early as the deadline has been extended once already. Three additional slots were made available during the announcement phase.

title: 31st GCMGC Announced with Team Registration Open
author: TNSA
content: The Tibetan National Sports Association has announced the 31st Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup (GCMGC), opening team registration for the next edition of the flagship tournament.
Following the historic 30th edition, the 31st GCMGC aims to build on the momentum with additional team slots being made available. Teams are encouraged to register before the deadline.

title: 36th Anniversary of Nobel Peace Prize Awarded to His Holiness
author: TNSA
content: TNSA marked the 36th anniversary of the Nobel Peace Prize awarded to His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama, honoring his unwavering commitment to non-violence and compassion.
As His Holiness has taught, 'My religion is simple. My religion is kindness.' These values continue to inspire the Tibetan sports community in their mission to unite and uplift through athletics.

title: GCM International Returns to New York in 2026
author: TNSA
content: The GCM International tournament is set to return to New York in 2026, bringing Tibetan football to the diaspora community in North America.
The announcement generated excitement among Tibetan football fans worldwide, with the tournament providing a platform for diaspora teams to compete and celebrate Tibetan sporting culture.

title: Team Tibet Competes in Sikkim Governor's Gold Cup 2025
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet was selected to compete in the prestigious Sikkim Governor's Gold Cup 2025, one of the oldest football tournaments in India.

The squad was finalized after rigorous selection, with a training camp held at Hunsur Rabgaylling Tibetan settlement with support from Rabling FC. The team received blessings from H.H. The Sakya Gongma Trichen before departing for Gangtok.
The first match pitted Team Tibet against the Sikkim champions in what was a significant milestone — competing in a mainstream Indian football tournament against established state-level teams.
The campaign reinforced Team Tibet's growing presence in competitive football circuits beyond the Tibetan diaspora community.

title: Inaugural Tongtsen Cup 2025: STS Dholanji and TCV Suja Crowned Champions
author: TNSA Media Team
content: The first edition of the Tongtsen Cup, an Under-15 boys and girls football tournament, kicked off at Upper TCV football ground, marking a major milestone in TNSA's grassroots football development program.

In the boys' category, STS Dholanji claimed the championship title, with TCV Gopalpur finishing as runners-up after a competitive final. The girls' category saw TCV Suja emerge as champions, defeating TCV Gopalpur in the final.

The tournament featured multiple days of group stage matches followed by semi-finals and finals, with all matches broadcast live on the TNSA Dharamsala YouTube channel. A Women Empowerment workshop was also organized for participants by the Women Empowerment Desk.

Chief Guest Mr. Jampa Phuntsok, Secretary of the Department of Health at the CTA, graced the event alongside other dignitaries. The Tongtsen Cup represents TNSA's commitment to developing young Tibetan athletes from the grassroots level.

title: Team Tibet Reaches Semi-Finals at Climate Cup 2025 in Ladakh
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet put on an impressive showing at the 3rd edition of the Climate Cup 2025, held in Ladakh, advancing all the way to the semi-finals before being eliminated.

The tournament brought together teams united by the cause of climate awareness, with matches played against the stunning backdrop of the Himalayan landscape. Team Tibet's matchday routines included traditional morning rituals that blended spiritual practice with athletic preparation.
Local Tibetan communities in Ladakh, including RTYC Leh and RTWA Ladakh, provided tremendous support throughout the tournament, making the players feel at home far from their base.

Though the team's journey ended in the semi-finals, the campaign highlighted the continued growth of Tibetan football on the competitive circuit. The Climate Cup serves as an important platform for Team Tibet to gain international match experience.

title: TNSA Strengthens Ties with Regional Football Associations
author: TNSA
content: TNSA extended its appreciation to partnerships with regional football bodies, including the Ladakh Football Association, as part of ongoing efforts to integrate Tibetan football into broader competitive frameworks.
These collaborations open doors for Team Tibet to participate in more regional tournaments and gain valuable competitive experience.

title: Team Tibet Participates in Independence Cup 2025 in Kalimpong
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet competed in the Independence Cup 2025 held in Kalimpong, using the tournament as part of their ongoing preparation for international competitions.
The team advanced through the group stages and competed in the quarter-finals against Share FC. The Kalimpong Tibetan community provided warm hospitality throughout the tournament.

A meeting was held between TNSA Executive Director Jangchup Gyaltsen and Mr. Menla Ethenpa, President of the Sikkim Football Association, strengthening ties between Tibetan football and regional football governance.

title: Trungkar Cup 2025 Held at Tongtsen Football Academy
author: TNSA
content: The Trungkar Cup 2025 was held at the Tongtsen Football Academy in Dehradun, continuing TNSA's efforts to provide regular competitive football opportunities for Tibetan players.
The tournament brought together teams in a competitive environment, further strengthening the domestic football ecosystem within the Tibetan community.

title: TNSA Celebrates His Holiness the Dalai Lama's 90th Birthday
author: TNSA
content: On the sacred occasion of His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama's 90th birthday, the Tibetan National Sports Association joined the global Tibetan community in celebrating the life and teachings of Tibet's spiritual leader.
TNSA acknowledged the profound impact of His Holiness's message of compassion, non-violence, and unity — values that are at the heart of Tibetan sports and community building.

title: Team Tibet Competes in CONIFA Men's Asia Cup 2025 in London
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet traveled to London to compete in the CONIFA Men's Asia Cup 2025, representing the Tibetan diaspora on the international football stage.

The squad was carefully selected from top performers at the GCMGC 2025, evaluated on skill, fitness, and overall performance throughout the domestic season. Players from across the diaspora — including the US, Canada, India, and Europe — came together to wear the national colors.

The team's journey included matches against other stateless and unrecognized nations competing under the CONIFA banner. Despite the challenges of assembling a diaspora team, the players demonstrated unity and determination on the pitch.

TNSA organized a comprehensive preparation program including training camps and friendly matches leading up to the tournament. The London Tibetan community and London Tibetan Football Club provided crucial support during the team's stay.
Team Tibet also participated in the Ghoton Cup 2025 in London as part of their preparation, successfully lifting the trophy in that tournament.

title: Team Tibet Lifts the London Ghoton Cup 2025
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet triumphed at the London Ghoton Cup 2025, lifting the trophy in a celebration of Tibetan football talent abroad.
The Ghoton Cup, organized by the London Tibetan community and London Tibetan Football Club, brought together clubs from across Europe in a friendly yet competitive atmosphere.
The victory provided a significant morale boost for Team Tibet ahead of their CONIFA Asia Cup 2025 campaign, demonstrating the team's cohesion and quality against strong opposition.

title: DYSA Mundgod Wins the Historic 30th GCMGC
author: TNSA Media Team
content: DYSA Mundgod has been crowned champions of the historic 30th Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup (GCMGC), defeating TDL Bylakuppe in a thrilling final held at Upper TCV grounds.
TDL Bylakuppe, the defending champions, fought valiantly but finished as runners-up. The final drew large crowds and was broadcast live on the TNSA YouTube channel.

The milestone 30th edition featured 16 teams divided into four groups, including teams from across India and Nepal: Kathmandu Hope and Challenge FC, Gangtok FC, Delhi Samyeling, Shimla United, Shugar United, TCB United, and many more.

The tournament showcased top-level Tibetan football over 10 days of intense competition, featuring group stage matches, quarter-finals, semi-finals, and the grand final. TNSA also introduced eco-friendly initiatives with water refill stations at the grounds.

The event received special blessings from TNSA Executive Members and featured the launch of a Tibetan Football Terms Book created in collaboration with Central TNSA. New official Tibetan national jerseys were also made available for purchase during the tournament.
TNSA extends its deepest gratitude to TCV and Upper TCV for providing the venue and ensuring the smooth organization of this landmark 30th edition.

title: TNSA Announces Under-15 Boys & Girls Football Tournament
author: TNSA
content: The Tibetan National Sports Association announced an Under-15 Boys & Girls Football Tournament as part of its commitment to youth sports development.
The tournament aims to identify and nurture young talent while providing a competitive platform for Tibetan school-age athletes to develop their skills.

title: TNSA Unveils New 2025 Tibetan National Team Jersey
author: TNSA Media Team
content: The Tibetan National Sports Association unveiled the new 2025 Tibetan National Team Jersey in a multi-day reveal campaign that generated excitement across the community.
Described as bold, modern, and made for everyone who believes in the game and the nation, the jersey was designed to be worn with pride both on the pitch and in the stands.

The official jerseys were made available for purchase during the GCMGC at Upper TCV, with additional retail locations in Dharamsala (Tibet Museum), Delhi (Noryak Store), London (Stockport), France (Paris), and South Korea.
The jersey represents a new era for Tibetan national sports, blending modern athletic design with national pride and cultural identity.

title: 30th GCMGC 2025: Team Registration and Official Announcement
author: TNSA
content: TNSA officially announced the 30th Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup and opened team registration for the milestone edition.

The tournament attracted 16 teams from settlements across the diaspora. Registration forms were distributed and the updated team list was published as slots filled up, with the countdown building excitement across the Tibetan community.

title: Workshop for Physical Education Teachers Organized by TNSA
author: TNSA
content: A workshop for physical education teachers was organized by the Department of Education in collaboration with TNSA, aimed at improving sports instruction across Tibetan schools.
The workshop brought together PE teachers from various Tibetan schools to share best practices, learn new coaching methodologies, and align on standards for youth sports development.

title: TNSA Executive Members Meet to Shape Future of Tibetan Sports
author: TNSA
content: The TNSA Executive Members convened for a productive meeting focused on shaping the future direction of Tibetan sports.
Discussions covered upcoming tournament planning, grassroots development initiatives, international competition strategy, and organizational improvements to strengthen the Tibetan sports ecosystem.

title: Year-Round Basketball Camp Launched for Tibetan Youth
author: TNSA
content: TNSA launched a year-round basketball camp for Tibetan kids at the CTA basketball ground, expanding the association's reach beyond football into multi-sport development.
The camp provides regular training sessions for young athletes, fostering sportsmanship, teamwork, and physical fitness among Tibetan youth in the Dharamsala community.

title: TNSA Tongtsen Grassroot Football Academy Expands Programs
author: TNSA
content: The TNSA Tongtsen Grassroot Football Academy continues to expand its programs, running sessions at multiple locations including Lower TCV School ground, in collaboration with Tibetan Children's Village Selakui, and with the Wonder Years project for the Norling Young Stars Club.

The grassroots program aims to develop young Tibetan footballers from an early age, providing professional coaching and regular training opportunities. The academy represents TNSA's long-term investment in building a pipeline of talent for Tibetan football.

title: Chikdril Gold Cup 2025: Red Devils Win Boys Title, Munsel Takes Women's Crown
author: TNSA Media Team
content: The Chikdril Gold Cup 2025 (2nd edition) was held at Mundgod, featuring both boys' and women's categories in an expanding tournament format.

In the boys' category, Red Devils claimed the championship title, defeating Dhondupling Basketball Club who finished as runners-up. The women's category saw Munsel emerge as champions, with Ama United finishing as runners-up.
Best player awards were presented in both categories, recognizing outstanding individual performances throughout the tournament.
The Chikdril Gold Cup has quickly established itself as an important fixture in the Tibetan sports calendar, promoting competitive football at the community level.

title: First Tibetan U11 and U13 Youth Soccer Tournament Held in the US
author: TNSA
content: In a groundbreaking collaboration between TNSA Youth Soccer and Tibet FC Grassroots Soccer, the very first Tibetan U11 and U13 youth soccer tournament was held in the United States.

This historic event marks the expansion of Tibetan grassroots football development to the diaspora community in North America, providing young Tibetan-Americans with an opportunity to connect with their heritage through sport.

title: 17th Anniversary of Congressional Gold Medal Awarded to His Holiness
author: TNSA
content: TNSA commemorated the 17th anniversary of the prestigious U.S. Congressional Gold Medal awarded to His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama, reflecting on the recognition of his lifelong dedication to peace and human rights.

title: TNSA Holds Annual Board Meeting
author: TNSA
content: The Tibetan National Sports Association (TNSA) Board Members held their annual Board Meeting, bringing together stakeholders to review the year's activities and plan for the future of Tibetan sports.

The meeting covered key organizational matters, reviewed tournament outcomes, and set the strategic direction for upcoming events including the GCMGC, international competitions, and grassroots development programs.

title: Team Tibet Competes in Climate Cup 2024
author: TNSA Media Team
content: Team Tibet's participation in the Climate Cup 2024 concluded after a hard-fought campaign. Despite not achieving all objectives, the team showcased unwavering determination and spirit throughout the tournament.
The Climate Cup continues to serve as a valuable platform for Team Tibet to gain competitive international experience and raise awareness about both Tibetan identity and environmental causes.
